purpose: Aggregate axis distribution across today's Tier-1 promotions; surface over/under-representation, synonym risk, and diversity (Shannon, Gini).

intake.delta_explanation: 11 more witnesses than #29 final-rollup baseline — likely subsequent cycles emitted post-#29.

2_falsifiers_preregistered.claim: axis extraction reflects two honest views (strict per-item vs cycle-level dedup)
2_falsifiers_preregistered.evidence: both views reported separately, no silent merge

2_falsifiers_preregistered.claim: synonym clusters surfaced honestly, not silently collapsed
2_falsifiers_preregistered.evidence: explicit candidate list flagged but kept separate in counts
formulas inlined; cross-check Shannon_strict=3.892 vs max 4.000 (16 bins) consistent
interpretation section ties each claim to numbers

3_axes_fanout.role: compute + interpret entropy/Gini with honest confidence bound

five_op_kernel.4_tier_1_gate: F1+F2+F3 PASS for inventory; F4+F5 PASS for analysis -> all 5 PASS

schema_heterogeneity_finding.implication: Strict axis-per-promotion extraction undercounts; cycle-level axes (axes/axes_realized/axes_2/axes_fanout) is the richer canonical surface. Both reported.

axis_frequency_table_strict.description: axis field per Tier-1 promotion ITEM (only items inside tier_1_promotions/tier1_promotions/tier_1_findings lists)

axis_frequency_table_cycle_level.description: axes field per WITNESS (deduped within witness, sourced from axes/axes_declared/axes_realized/axes_2/axes_fanout/fanout_axes)

synonym_cluster_audit.policy: HEURISTIC clustering by shared head-token; NOT silent merging — counts kept separate.

explicit_candidates_flagged.verdict: likely same intent (counter-evidence honesty); kept separate pending author confirmation
audit suffix may denote distinct cycle-type (audit-vs-creation); kept separate
different framings (honesty vs correctness) — likely overlapping but not identical; kept separate
likely same with mechanics suffix; kept separate

synonym_cluster_audit.honest_caveat: Heuristic head-token clustering is NOT semantic equivalence proof. Counts above represent distinct labels that share a token, not collapsed merges.

diversity_metrics.confidence_bound: Sample is small (N_strict=19, N_cycle=99). Shannon entropy ±0.5 bits per kick policy guidance; Gini ±0.05. Numbers are descriptive, not inferential.

honest_interpretation.headline: HIGH diversity, near-uniform — axes are NOT concentrated; risk is the opposite (every cycle invents a new axis label)
honest_interpretation.evidence_strict: Top axis 'remote-infra-discovery' appears 3× of 19 (15.8%); next 'rc-propagation-correctness' 2× (10.5%); 11/16 axes are one-time (68.8%). Shannon 3.89/4.00 max ≈ 0.973 normalized — near-maximum diversity.
honest_interpretation.evidence_cycle: Top axis 'sweep-coverage' appears 4× of 49 witnesses (8.2%); next 'scope-honesty' 3× (6.1%). 50/62 distinct axes are one-time (80.6%). Shannon 5.86/5.95 max ≈ 0.984 normalized.
honest_interpretation.over_representation_finding: NONE. No axis exceeds 16% of strict observations or 9% of cycle observations. No 'over-reliance on a few framings' detected.
honest_interpretation.under_representation_finding: Several pre-named axes from Background (design-strategy, falsifier-coverage, env-gate-resolution) appear ≤ 1-2× — they are NOT over-used either. Genuine canonical axes do not yet exist.
honest_interpretation.primary_risk: Axis-name proliferation, not axis concentration. 50+ one-time labels suggest authors mint fresh axis names per cycle rather than reusing canonical ones — schema-uniformity problem (cross-link #20), not framing-monoculture problem.
honest_interpretation.synonym_risk_quantified: At least 5 explicit candidate-synonym pairs detected; if collapsed, distinct-axis count drops by ~5-8 but Shannon entropy remains ≥ 5.7 bits. Even after canonicalization, diversity stays high.
honest_interpretation.comparison_to_concern_in_kick: Kick asked 'are some axes over-represented?' — answer: NO. Kick asked 'which axes deserve more cycles?' — answer: ALL of them; the methodology is to converge axis vocabulary, not to rebalance frequencies.

cross_links.to_29_session_final_rollup_extended: Background cite — baseline ~36 Tier-1 / ~38 cycles; this kick observed 49 witnesses & 99 cycle-level Tier-1 count (richer extraction)
cross_links.to_20_design_kick_witness_schema_uniform: Schema heterogeneity (32/49 witnesses lack tier_1_promotions array) is the same finding axis_inventory had to navigate here.
cross_links.to_25_at_origin_block_presence_audit_cro: Axis-name proliferation parallels @origin-tag drift — naming discipline gap is systemic.

carry_forward: Canonicalize axis vocabulary: produce CANONICAL_AXES.md (≤ 20 entries) merging clear synonyms; add lint to design/kick witnesses to warn on novel axis label.
Schema convergence (carry from #20): make tier_1_promotions[] with axis field MANDATORY rather than optional.
Re-run this distribution after canonicalization to compare pre/post Shannon/Gini delta.
carry_forward_origin: session-final-rollup-extended-2026-04-27 (#29) -> baseline Tier-1 axis tagging norm; this kick MT1-self-applies that norm to a meta-stat layer.
@methodology_self_application_note: MT1: cited #29 as parent. This cycle is a stat layer over its outputs.
sentinels: __TIER_1_AXES_DIST__ PASS tier1_total=99 distinct_axes=62 top1_axis_count=4 shannon_entropy=5.857 gini=0.117
